metadata.note: All 3 evals run via live delegation subagent executor + main-agent grader. No analytical stubs.

expectations.text: Response identifies all three data sources (Hebcal, AlAdhan, algorithm)
expectations.evidence: Hebcal, AlAdhan, and Computus algorithm all named

expectations.text: Response provides or references the ObservanceEvent unified interface
expectations.evidence: Full ObservanceEvent TypeScript interface provided with all fields

expectations.text: Response shows the parallel fetch pattern using Promise.allSettled
expectations.evidence: Executor used Promise.all not Promise.allSettled despite skill containing WARNING. Fixed: WARNING box added to skill body.

expectations.text: Response mentions that all three sources are free and require no API key
expectations.evidence: Hebcal: no API key. AlAdhan: free endpoint. Computus: client-side algorithm.

expectations.text: Response identifies all three data sources (Hebcal, AlAdhan, algorithm)
expectations.evidence: Recommended Calendarific API (PAID, requires API key) for Christian holidays instead of free Computus algorithm

expectations.text: Response provides or references the ObservanceEvent unified interface
expectations.evidence: Used 'ReligiousEvent' interface (not ObservanceEvent) with different field types

expectations.text: Response shows the parallel fetch pattern using Promise.allSettled
expectations.evidence: Used Promise.all

expectations.text: Response mentions that all three sources are free and require no API key
expectations.evidence: Calendarific shown with api_key=YOUR_API_KEY -- requires paid subscription

expectations.text: Response gives the correct Western Easter 2027 date: March 28
expectations.evidence: 'March 28, 2027' confirmed

expectations.text: Response gives the correct Orthodox Easter 2027 date: May 2
expectations.evidence: 'May 2, 2027' confirmed

expectations.text: Response provides or references the Computus algorithm functions
expectations.evidence: Both computeWesternEaster and computeOrthodoxEaster provided with full implementations

expectations.evidence: 'Julian Computus with Gregorian offset (+13 days for the 20th/21st century)'

expectations.text: Response gives the correct Western Easter 2027 date: March 28
expectations.evidence: 'March 28, 2027' -- correct from LLM training data

expectations.evidence: 'May 2, 2027' -- correct from LLM training data

expectations.text: Response provides or references the Computus algorithm functions
expectations.evidence: No function names or TypeScript implementations -- plain prose explanation

expectations.evidence: Mentions Julian calendar but not the specific 13-day offset mechanism

expectations.text: Response provides or references the getHolidayDescription function
expectations.evidence: async function getHolidayDescription(rawName: string) provided with full implementation

expectations.evidence: 'GET https://en.wikipedia.org/api/rest_v1/page/summary/{article_title}'

expectations.evidence: WIKIPEDIA_ARTICLE_MAP shown with 'Rosh Hashanah'/'Rosh Hashana' entries handling both spellings

expectations.evidence: 'Attribution Text: Description via Wikipedia (CC BY-SA 3.0)' -- exact match

expectations.evidence: No WIKIPEDIA_ARTICLE_MAP or equivalent lookup structure mentioned

expectations.evidence: Says 'From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia' -- different format, missing explicit CC BY-SA 3.0 license

notes: All 3 evals: LIVE delegation subagent runs
Eval 1 with_skill 0.75: Promise.allSettled gap found -- executor used Promise.all. WARNING box added to skill.
Eval 1 without_skill 0.0: recommended Calendarific (PAID) -- highest-consequence failure in all 15 without-skill evals
Eval 2 without_skill 0.5: Easter dates correct from training data but no Computus function code
